actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Individuals who build, deploy, and maintain applications or contribute to the Hyperlane protocol. (doc_1, doc_2, doc_4, doc_17, doc_21, doc_31, doc_45)

actors.name: User
actors.description: End-users interacting with applications built on Hyperlane, for example, transferring tokens. (doc_3, doc_21)
capabilities: Use public digital wallet address for interactions (doc_3)

actors.name: Relayer
actors.description: Off-chain agent responsible for relaying messages between blockchains by indexing messages on an origin chain Mailbox and submitting them to the destination chain's Mailbox. (doc_10, doc_21)
capabilities: Index messages from Mailbox on origin chain (doc_21, doc_10)
Process messages on destination chain Mailbox (doc_21, doc_10)

actors.name: Validator
actors.description: Off-chain agent responsible for observing messages and providing security attestations for Interchain Security Modules. (doc_10, doc_13)
capabilities: Provide security attestations (doc_10, doc_19 for ISMs)
Connect to home chain and replicas (doc_10)
Run as a node on AWS (example setup) (doc_13)

actors.name: Scraper Agent
actors.description: Off-chain agent that collects and stores data from blockchains, typically into a database like Postgres. (doc_10, doc_11)

actors.name: Maintainer/Core Dev
actors.description: Individuals with privileged access to review and merge contributions to the Hyperlane codebase. (doc_2)

actors.name: GCP Service Account
actors.description: A Google Cloud Platform service account used by systems like 'external-secrets' to access GCP resources, such as Secret Manager. (doc_43)

actors.name: Github Proxy
actors.description: A CloudFlare Worker that proxies Github API requests using an API key to allow higher rate limits. (doc_37)

actors.name: CI System
actors.description: Continuous Integration system (e.g., GitHub Actions) that automates building, testing, and deployment processes. (doc_4)
capabilities: Build Docker images for agents (doc_4, doc_10)
Run automated tests (doc_4, doc_10 for E2E tests)
Deploy code changes (doc_4, doc_10 for agent deployment procedures)

actors.name: Message Sender
actors.description: An entity (EOA or smart contract) that initiates a message dispatch through the Mailbox. (doc_18, solidity/contracts/hooks/README.md)
capabilities: Specify destination, recipient, and message body (doc_18, general Mailbox functionality)

actors.name: Message Recipient
actors.description: A smart contract on a destination chain that is designated to receive and handle an interchain message. (doc_19, solidity/contracts/isms/README.md)
capabilities: Define an InterchainSecurityModule for message verification (doc_19)

actors.name: Deployer (Interchain Account)
actors.description: The account on an origin chain that deploys and exclusively controls an Interchain Account on a remote chain. (doc_20, solidity/contracts/middleware/README.md)
capabilities: Control Interchain Accounts on remote chains (doc_20)

actors.name: Hyperlane CLI User
actors.description: A developer or operator using the Hyperlane Command Line Interface. (doc_31)

actors.name: Hyperlane SDK User
actors.description: A developer using the Hyperlane Software Development Kit to build applications. (doc_45)
capabilities: Manage chain metadata and RPC providers (doc_45)

actors.name: CCIP Relayer
actors.description: A relayer specific to the CCIP (Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol) context, interacting with the CCIP-read service framework. (doc_29)
capabilities: Make POST requests to CCIP Server with ABI-encoded data (doc_29)

actors.name: Kubernetes Workload
actors.description: Applications or services running on a Kubernetes cluster that may need to access secrets. (doc_43)
capabilities: Access secrets from GCP Secret Manager via ExternalSecret and ClusterSecretStore (doc_43)

assets.name: Arbitrary Interchain Data
assets.description: General data sent between blockchains via the Hyperlane Protocol. (doc_3)

assets.name: Digital Assets / Tokens
assets.description: Cryptocurrency tokens or other digital assets transferred or managed by the Hyperlane Protocol. (doc_3, doc_21)

assets.name: Shared State
assets.description: Application state shared between blockchains using Hyperlane. (doc_4)

assets.name: Interchain Messages
assets.description: The payload and metadata of messages dispatched through the Mailbox. (doc_4, doc_18, doc_19)

assets.name: Source Code
assets.description: The codebase of the Hyperlane protocol and related tools. (doc_2, doc_4, doc_17, etc.)

assets.name: Docker Images
assets.description: Container images for Hyperlane agents. (doc_4, doc_10)

assets.name: NPM Packages
assets.description: Published software packages for Hyperlane SDK, CLI, core, etc. (doc_4, doc_17, doc_30, doc_44)

assets.name: Contract Addresses
assets.description: Deployed addresses of Hyperlane smart contracts. (doc_10, doc_31 `hyperlane chains addresses`)

assets.name: Configuration Files
assets.description: Files containing settings for agents, deployments, and other components. (doc_4, doc_10, doc_31)

assets.name: Environment Variables
assets.description: Configuration variables, potentially including secrets, used by Hyperlane components. (doc_4 `LOG_LEVEL`, doc_10 `HYP_DB`)

assets.name: Secrets (GCP Secret Manager)
assets.description: Sensitive data like API keys or credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager. (doc_43)

assets.name: Service Account Keys
assets.description: Credentials for service accounts, e.g., for GCP. (doc_10 `GCS_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY`, doc_43 `gcp-sa-secret.yaml`)

assets.name: Database Data (Scraper)
assets.description: Data collected by the Scraper agent and stored in its database. (doc_11)

assets.name: Contract Artifacts
assets.description: Compiled bytecode and ABI of smart contracts. (doc_16, doc_17, doc_23, doc_24)

assets.name: ERC20 Tokens
assets.description: Fungible tokens adhering to the ERC20 standard, managed by Warp Routes. (doc_21)

assets.name: ERC721 Tokens
assets.description: Non-fungible tokens adhering to the ERC721 standard, managed by Warp Routes. (doc_21)

assets.name: Native Assets
assets.description: Native blockchain assets (e.g., ETH) warped via Hyperlane. (doc_21)

assets.name: Yield-bearing Assets (ERC4626)
assets.description: Assets that generate yield, transferred across chains using ERC4626 standard via Yield Routes. (doc_22)

assets.name: Vault Shares (ERC4626)
assets.description: Shares representing a user's deposit in an ERC4626 vault. (doc_22)

assets.name: Exchange Rate Data
assets.description: Exchange rate information used in Yield Routes for ERC4626 assets. (doc_22)

assets.name: Message Nonce
assets.description: A number used once, often for replay protection or ordering in messaging systems. (doc_22 `nonce += 1`)

assets.name: Public Digital Wallet Address
assets.description: User's public blockchain address used for interactions. (doc_3)

assets.name: Github API Key
assets.description: API key for authenticating with the Github API, used by Github Proxy. (doc_37)

assets.name: TokenMessage
assets.description: Encoded payload for token transfers containing amount and recipient. (doc_21)

assets.name: Monitoring Dashboard Templates
assets.description: JSON templates for Grafana dashboards used for monitoring Hyperlane. (doc_25)

components.name: Hyperlane Protocol
components.description: An open generalized interchain messaging protocol. (doc_3)

components.name: Mailbox (Solidity EVM)
components.description: Core smart contract on EVM chains for dispatching and receiving interchain messages. Manages interactions with Hooks and ISMs. (doc_17, doc_18, doc_19)

components.name: Mailbox (Sealevel SVM)
components.description: Core smart contract on Sealevel (Solana Virtual Machine) chains for interchain messaging. (doc_10, doc_14)

components.name: Post-Dispatch Hook (IPostDispatchHook)
components.description: Interface for smart contracts that define additional origin chain behavior for dispatched messages. Can charge fees. (doc_18)

components.name: Interchain Security Module (ISM)
components.description: Smart contracts that define additional security checks for incoming messages on the destination chain. (doc_19)

components.name: TokenRouter (Solidity)
components.description: A collection of contracts for Hyperlane Warp Routes, enabling interchain token transfers (ERC20, ERC721, Native). (doc_21)

components.name: HypNative
components.description: TokenRouter flavor for warping native assets from their canonical chain. (doc_21)

components.name: HypERC20Collateral
components.description: TokenRouter flavor for warping ERC20 or ERC721 tokens from their canonical chain. (doc_21)

components.name: HypERC20 (Synthetic)
components.description: TokenRouter flavor for representing warped tokens (Native, ERC20, ERC721) on a non-canonical chain. (doc_21)

components.name: HypERC4626Collateral
components.description: Contract for Yield Routes; implements ERC4626 deposits upon transfer and withdrawal upon transfer back. (doc_22)

components.name: HypERC4626 (Synthetic)
components.description: Contract for Yield Routes; implements a rebasing ERC20 representing shares in an ERC4626 vault on the collateral chain. (doc_22)

components.name: Interchain Account (ICA)
components.description: Smart contract deployed on a remote chain, controlled by an origin chain deployer account, offering a transparent multicall API. (doc_20)

components.name: Interchain Query System (IQS)
components.description: System generalizing view calls to contracts on remote chains via a transparent multicall API. (doc_20)

components.name: Hyperlane Core (Solidity Package)
components.description: Package containing contracts and typechain artifacts for Hyperlane EVM implementation. (doc_17)

components.name: Hyperlane Starknet Core (Cairo/TS Package)
components.description: Package providing TypeScript tooling and Cairo contract artifacts for Hyperlane on Starknet. (doc_24)

components.name: Hyperlane CLI (Command Line Interface)
components.description: Typescript tool for deploying Hyperlane core contracts and warp routes, sending messages, etc. (doc_31)

components.name: Hyperlane SDK (Software Development Kit)
components.description: Typescript library for developers to create and manage interchain applications. (doc_45)

components.name: CCIP-read Service Framework
components.description: Service framework for CCIP-read, allowing execution logic based on Hyperlane Relayer calls. (doc_29)

components.name: CCIP Server
components.description: Main entry point for the CCIP-read service framework, handles requests from CCIP Relayers. (doc_29)

components.name: Hyperlane Cosmos Module SDK
components.description: TypeScript SDK for the Cosmos Hyperlane Module Implementation. (doc_33)

components.name: Hyperlane Infra Scripts
components.description: Scripts and utilities for managing and deploying Hyperlane infrastructure. (doc_42)

components.name: External Secrets Helm Chart (GCP)
components.description: Helm chart to allow Kubernetes workloads to access secrets from GCP Secret Manager. (doc_43)

components.name: Hyperlane Widgets
components.description: Common React components for projects using Hyperlane, e.g., ChainLogo, MessageTimeline. (doc_50)

components.name: Rust Agents (Relayer, Validator, Scraper)
components.description: Off-chain programs written in Rust that manage Hyperlane operations. (doc_10)

components.name: ERC20 Contract (Generic)
components.description: A standard ERC20 token contract. (doc_21, doc_22)

components.name: ERC4626 Vault Contract (Generic)
components.description: A standard ERC4626 tokenized vault contract. (doc_22)

components.name: GCP Secret Manager
components.description: Google Cloud Platform service for storing secrets. (doc_43)

components.name: Postgres Database (Scraper)
components.description: PostgreSQL database used by the Scraper agent. (doc_11)

components.name: Github API
components.description: External API for Github services. (doc_37, doc_24)

components.name: Hyperlane Registry
components.description: A source for Hyperlane chain metadata, contract addresses, and configurations. (doc_10 `hyperlane-registry` repo, doc_30 CLI interaction with registry)

components.name: Terraform Module for Hyperlane Validator
components.description: Terraform module to set up the necessary infrastructure for a Hyperlane validator on AWS. It automates the creation of resources such as ECS clusters, VPCs, subnets, and security groups required for running a validator node. (doc_13)

components.name: Grafana Dashboards
components.description: JSON model templates for Grafana dashboards, used for monitoring Hyperlane. These are imported/exported manually. (doc_25)

components.name: Scraper Migrator CLI
components.description: Command Line Interface (cargo run --package migration) for managing scraper PostgreSQL database schema migrations, including initializing the DB, recreating it, and generating SeaORM entities. (doc_11, doc_12)

data_flows.data: Dispatch call (destination, recipient, body, metadata, hook, value)
data_flows.description: Message Sender dispatches a message via the EVM Mailbox. (doc_18)
EVM Mailbox calls a Post-Dispatch Hook. (doc_18)
EVM Mailbox calls an ISM on the destination chain to verify a message. (doc_19)
EVM Mailbox delivers a verified message to the recipient contract. (doc_19)
User initiates a remote token transfer via TokenRouter. (doc_21)
TokenRouter dispatches a TokenMessage via the Mailbox. (doc_21)
Relayer submits a message from an origin chain to the destination Mailbox. (doc_21, doc_10)
HypERC20Collateral interacts with the underlying ERC20 token contract. (doc_21 diagram)
HypERC4626Collateral interacts with an ERC4626 vault. (doc_22 diagram)

data_flows.data: Dispatch call (TokenMessage: recipient, shares, exchangeRate, nonce)
data_flows.description: HypERC4626Collateral dispatches yield route message via Mailbox. (doc_22 diagram)
Scraper agent writes collected blockchain data to Postgres database. (doc_11)
Github Proxy makes authenticated requests to Github API. (doc_37)
External Secrets Helm Chart (via ClusterSecretStore) accesses secrets from GCP Secret Manager. (doc_43)

data_flows.data: POST Request with ABI-encoded data (sender, data)
data_flows.description: CCIP Relayer sends request to CCIP Server. (doc_29)
Developer uses Hyperlane CLI to manage deployments. (doc_31)
Hyperlane CLI deploys or configures Mailbox contract. (doc_31 `deploy core`)
Hyperlane SDK fetches configuration from Hyperlane Registry. (doc_44 SDK uses registry, doc_30 CLI uses registry)

trust_boundaries.name: User-to-SmartContract
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between end-users (EOAs) and Hyperlane smart contracts. Interactions are typically transactions. (General blockchain interaction model)

trust_boundaries.name: Developer-to-Tooling
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between developers and Hyperlane CLI/SDK. (doc_31, doc_45)

trust_boundaries.name: OffChainAgent-to-SmartContract
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between off-chain agents (Relayer, Validator) and Hyperlane smart contracts (Mailbox). Scraper reads from chain but direct interactive boundary with Mailbox is less prominent for write/control actions. (doc_10, doc_19, doc_21)

trust_boundaries.name: Inter-Chain Communication
trust_boundaries.description: Logical boundary representing communication between Mailbox contracts on different chains, facilitated by Relayers and secured by ISMs. (doc_4, doc_10)

trust_boundaries.name: Hyperlane-to-ExternalServices
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between Hyperlane system components and external services like Github (via Github Proxy), GCP Secret Manager. (doc_37, doc_43)

trust_boundaries.name: SmartContract-to-SmartContract (Internal)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary for interactions between different Hyperlane smart contracts on the same chain (e.g., Mailbox to Hook/ISM, TokenRouter to Mailbox). (doc_18, doc_19, doc_21)

trust_boundaries.name: CCIP_Relayer-to-CCIP_Server
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between CCIP Relayer and the CCIP-read service framework server. (doc_29)

trust_boundaries.name: Kubernetes-to-GCP_SecretManager
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between Kubernetes workloads and GCP Secret Manager, mediated by External Secrets. (doc_43)

behaviors.name: Dispatch Interchain Message
behaviors.description: A Message Sender initiates an interchain message by calling the dispatch function on the Mailbox contract. (doc_18)

behaviors.name: Relay Message
behaviors.description: A Relayer submits a message, observed on an origin chain, to the Mailbox contract on a destination chain. (doc_21, doc_10)

behaviors.name: Verify Message (ISM)
behaviors.description: The Mailbox contract calls the verify function on a configured Interchain Security Module for an incoming message. (doc_19)

behaviors.name: Handle Message
behaviors.description: The Mailbox contract calls the handle function on the designated Message Recipient contract after successful verification. (doc_19)

behaviors.name: Transfer Remote Tokens
behaviors.description: A User initiates an interchain token transfer by calling transferRemote on a TokenRouter contract. (doc_21)

behaviors.name: Deploy Core Contracts
behaviors.description: A Developer uses the Hyperlane CLI to deploy core Hyperlane contracts to a blockchain. (doc_31)

behaviors.name: Deploy Warp Route
behaviors.description: A Developer uses the Hyperlane CLI to deploy a Warp Route. (doc_31)

behaviors.name: Call Post-Dispatch Hook
behaviors.description: The Mailbox contract calls a configured Post-Dispatch Hook after a message is dispatched. (doc_18)

behaviors.name: Mint Synthetic Token
behaviors.description: A Synthetic TokenRouter (e.g., HypERC20) mints tokens for the recipient on the destination chain. (doc_21 diagram)

behaviors.name: Burn Synthetic Token
behaviors.description: A Synthetic TokenRouter burns tokens from a user when transferring back to a canonical chain. (doc_21 diagram)

behaviors.name: Deposit to ERC4626 Vault
behaviors.description: HypERC4626Collateral deposits assets into an ERC4626 vault during a remote transfer. (doc_22 diagram)

behaviors.name: Redeem from ERC4626 Vault
behaviors.description: HypERC4626Collateral redeems assets from an ERC4626 vault when handling an incoming transfer. (doc_22 diagram)

behaviors.name: Fetch Secret from GCP
behaviors.description: External Secrets Helm Chart, through ClusterSecretStore and GCP Service Account, fetches a secret from GCP Secret Manager. (doc_43)

behaviors.name: Submit Attestation
behaviors.description: A Validator provides security attestations to an Interchain Security Module. (doc_10, doc_19)

behaviors.name: Scrape Blockchain Data
behaviors.description: Scraper agent reads data from blockchains and writes it to a Postgres database. (doc_11)

behaviors.name: Proxy Github API Request
behaviors.description: A client (e.g., developer tool using Hyperlane Starknet Core (doc_24)) requests Github data via the Github Proxy. (doc_37)
